notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +17.5% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.note: hero-attach conditional, modeled as hitting (sim convention)

augments.note: extra missile per attack; per-missile damage share unstated

augments.name: Suppressive Fire
augments.note: new 1.15 hero augment; effect not yet modeled by the sim

metaBuilds.title: Crit Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 924 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 1% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Crit DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 356 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +28% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Plasma Blade (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Crit Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 413 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +51% more 10s rotation damage: try Viper over Plasma Blade (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Sparrow ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. TwinBlast ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Eden ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Assault MK-II ” ⇒ poke; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field carry 49.6% over 1,533 games, magnitude not simulated.
